courseTitle: Annotations & Dimensions — Municipal Drawing Best Practices
courseDescription: Beginner-friendly guide to text, dimensioning, leaders, and annotation styles for clear municipal drawings (Pokhara-friendly conventions). Focuses on annotative scaling, dimension styles, and print-ready settings.
courseObjectives: Create consistent text and dimension styles for municipal submissions
Use annotative objects and scale-aware annotations
Dimension plans accurately with best-practice settings
Produce print-ready PDF drawings with correct annotation sizes

phases.phaseTitle: Setup — Units, Text Style, and Annotation Scaling
phases.phaseDescription: Prepare your drawing with correct units and scalable annotation styles.

steps.title: Set Drawing Units and Precision
steps.subtitle: UNITS command — match municipality preferences
steps.why: Wrong units or precision lead to rejected drawings and incorrect dimensions.
instructions: Type UNITS and set Type to Decimal, Insertion Scale to Millimeters (or Meters if used).
Set Precision to match municipal expectations (e.g., 0.00 for cm-level, 0.000 for mm-level).
Save as a template once configured for reuse.

steps.title: Create Text and Dimension Styles
steps.subtitle: Define `TEXT` and `DIMSTYLE` templates
steps.why: Consistent text height and dimension appearance are mandatory for professional submissions.
instructions: Open `STYLE`, create a style named TXT_MUNI: set font, height (e.g., 3.5 mm for plan text at 1:100), and orientation.
Open `DIMSTYLE`, create MUNI_DIM: set text height (2.5-3.5 mm), arrow size (2.5 mm), and units format to match UNITS.
Enable Annotative for styles when you plan to use multiple viewport scales.

note.label: Info
note.text: Store these styles in your office template (.dwt) for consistent reuse.

phases.phaseDescription: Set up dimension rules so distances, radii, and angles appear correctly and consistently.

steps.title: Create and Modify Dimension Style
steps.subtitle: DIMSTYLE → New / Modify
steps.why: A single dimension style ensures all dims follow municipal rules and are readable at print scale.
instructions: Type DIMSTYLE → New → MUNI_DIM → Continue.
In Fit tab choose Text placement (Above Dimension Line) and set Text gap to 1.5 mm.
Set Primary units to match UNITS (decimal), precision appropriate to project (0.00 or 0.000).
Set Arrowheads to a consistent size (2.5 mm) and string format for tolerances if required.

practicalExample.note: Use Annotative dims when using model/layout scaling.

steps.title: Dimensioning Commands — Aligned, Linear, and Angular
steps.subtitle: Use the right command for the right measurement
steps.why: Incorrect dimension types cause misinterpretation during review.
instructions: Use DIMALIGNED for slanted walls and perimeter distances — it measures the true length between two points.
Use DIMLINEAR for orthogonal widths and heights (X or Y direction).
Use DIMANGULAR for corner angles, and DIMRADIUS / DIMDIAMETER for arcs and circles.
Place dimension text outside the object for clarity and avoid overlapping other annotations.

phases.phaseDescription: Create scalable annotations that maintain readable size across viewports and scales.

steps.title: Create Annotative Text (MText) and Styles
steps.subtitle: Use annotative text for multiple viewport scales
steps.why: Annotative text automatically scales for different viewport scales, preventing manual resizing errors.
instructions: Create MText with the TXT_MUNI style and enable Annotative property in the Text Style settings.
When inserting, set the annotation scale (1:100, 1:200) so text appears correctly in each viewport.
Use ANNOALL to convert existing text to annotative if needed.

note.label: Warning
note.text: Avoid mixing annotative and non-annotative text in the same label.

steps.title: Use Multileader (QLEADER) for Notes
steps.subtitle: Create readable callouts for materials, north arrows, legends
steps.why: Leaders keep notes connected to objects without cluttering the drawing.
instructions: Type QLEADER and click the object, then place the leader text on TEXT layer using an annotative MText.
For repeated standard notes (e.g., 'Existing tree to remain'), create a block with leader and insert it where needed.
Keep leader arrowheads consistent and use the same leader landing distances for neatness.

phases.phaseDescription: Produce final drawing sheets with correctly scaled annotations and viewports.

steps.title: Create Layout and Viewports
steps.subtitle: Paper Space workflows for plotting
steps.why: Layouts let you present multiple scales and views on the same sheet with consistent annotation sizes.
instructions: Create a Layout, insert a viewport (MVIEW) and set its scale to 1:100 or municipality-required scale.
Ensure Annotative text/dims are set to the same scale so they display correctly in the viewport.
Lock viewport after setting scale to prevent accidental rescale.

steps.title: Plot and PDF Export Settings
steps.subtitle: Use PRINT/EXPORTPDF with the right settings
steps.why: Municipal reviewers expect PDFs with readable text and true lineweights.
instructions: Before plotting, restore a PRINT_STATE with only printable layers visible.
Use Plot Preview to verify lineweights and text legibility at final scale.
Export to PDF with vector output; embed fonts if required by municipality.

note.label: Success
note.text: Always submit a PDF sample at the municipal scale for quick review.

steps.title: Dimension Text Overlaps or Disappears
steps.subtitle: Check text height, scale, and fit settings
steps.why: Small or overlapped text causes reviewers to misread measurements.
instructions: If dim text overlaps, open DIMSTYLE → Fit → adjust text placement or scale.
If text disappears in viewport, ensure the annotative scale matches viewport scale and that objects are annotative.
Use QSELECT to find text with incorrect styles and change them to TXT_MUNI.

steps.title: Units or Decimal Mismatch
steps.subtitle: Verify UNITS and DIMSTYLE settings
steps.why: Mismatched units lead to numeric errors and rework.
instructions: Check UNITS → Insertion Scale and Primary Units in DIMSTYLE are consistent.
Update template if municipality changes unit preferences.
